expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report contains a Time Horizon for the recommendation
Report has an Executive Summary section
expectations.evidence: Line 29: '## Executive Summary' with coherent thesis paragraph

expectations.text: Report has a Quality Metrics Dashboard with Piotroski F-Score, Altman Z-Score, ROIC, and FCF Yield
expectations.evidence: Lines 35-42: Dashboard table with F-Score 6/9, Z-Score 9.40, ROIC 28.79%, FCF Yield 2.55%

expectations.text: Report has a Valuation Summary section with P/E, EV/EBITDA, or P/S multiples
expectations.evidence: Lines 46-52: Valuation table with P/E (FWD) 23.33x, EV/EBITDA 17.54x, P/S 9.95x

expectations.text: Report includes an intrinsic value estimate or margin of safety calculation
Report has a Scenario Analysis with bull, base, and bear cases
expectations.evidence: Lines 58-65: Scenario table with Bull/Base/Bear cases and probability weights

expectations.text: Report has an Options Analysis section
expectations.evidence: Lines 87-98: Options Analysis section present, though IV Rank and IV Percentile are both N/A

expectations.text: Report contains actual current MSFT price data (not fabricated)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'Current Price: $409.41' — matches with_skill output and real data

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
Report includes comparison to market benchmarks (S&P 500 or Nasdaq)

claims.claim: MSFT FY26 Q2 revenue was $81.3B (+17% YoY)

claims.evidence: Old skill failed to retrieve IV rank data from its data sources

suggestions.assertion: Report has an Options Analysis section
suggestions.reason: This passed despite IV Rank and IV Percentile both being N/A. The old skill couldn't retrieve options data, but still 'has' the section. A stricter assertion checking for actual numeric IV data would differentiate.
Both skills produce nearly identical fundamental data but differ in options completeness and recommendation quality — assertions don't capture this difference.

eval_feedback.overall: All 13 assertions pass for both configurations. The assertions are structurally sound but non-discriminating — they verify template compliance, not analytical depth. Key quality differences (IV data completeness, strategy specificity, recommendation rigor) go unmeasured.
